Project GDP Supplemental

Load New Packages!

New Data Joining!

clean_country <- function(x) {
  x |>
    stri_trans_general("Latin-ASCII") |>
    str_to_lower() |>
    str_replace_all("&", "and") |>
    str_remove_all("[[:punct:]]") |>
    str_squish()
}

gdp_web_clean <- gdp_web |>
  rename(year = scraped_year) |>
  mutate(
    year = as.numeric(year),
    country_join = recode(country,
      "United States" = "United States of America",
      "Bolivia" = "Bolivia (Plurinational State of)",
      "Venezuela" = "Venezuela (Bolivarian Republic of)"
    ),
    country_key = clean_country(country_join)
  )

cases_year_clean <- cases_year |>
  mutate(
    year = as.numeric(year),
    country_key = clean_country(country)
  )

cases_year_gdp <- cases_year_clean |>
  left_join(
    gdp_web_clean |>
      select(country_key, year, gdp_per_capita),
    by = c("country_key", "year")
  )

# join the yearly gdp/cases data to the monthly cases data
cases_gdp <- cases_month |>
  left_join(
    cases_year_gdp,
    by = c("country", "year"),
    suffix = c("_month", "_year")
  ) |>
  # make a tibble to join (threw an error without this)
  as_tibble()

Data Visualizations

Preparing data for visualization by removing year not available, renaming region acronyms for their representative names, and removing missing data.

# create yearly country-level dataset with GDP joined
cases_gdp_year <- cases_year |>
  filter(year != 2025) |>
  mutate(
    year = as.numeric(year),
    measles_epi_linked = as.numeric(measles_epi_linked)
  ) |>
  left_join(
    gdp_web |>
      rename(year = scraped_year) |>
      select(country, year, gdp_per_capita),
    by = c("country", "year")
  ) |>
  mutate(
    region = recode(region,
      "AFRO" = "Africa",
      "AMRO" = "Americas",
      "EMRO" = "Eastern Mediterranean",
      "EURO" = "Europe",
      "SEARO" = "South-East Asia",
      "WPRO" = "Western Pacific"
    )
  ) |>
  filter(
    !is.na(measles_epi_linked),
    !is.na(gdp_per_capita),
    measles_epi_linked > 0
  )

GDP per capita Visual 1

# faceted plot by region
ggplot(cases_gdp_year,
       aes(x = year,
           y = measles_epi_linked,
           color = gdp_per_capita)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.9, size = 3, width = 0.25, height = 0.1) +
  scale_y_log10(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2012, 2016, 2020, 2024)) +
  scale_color_gradient(
    low = "#A6DAF5",
    high = "#3E3F3A",
    labels = scales::comma
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ region, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Epidemiologically Linked Measles Cases Over Time by Region",
    subtitle = "Each point represents a country-year; color shows GDP per capita",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Epidemiologically linked measles cases (log scale)",
    color = "GDP per capita"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 25)

GDP per capita Visual 2 and 3

epi_region_plot <- cases_gdp_year |>
  ggplot(aes(x = log1p(measles_epi_linked),
             y = region,
             fill = ..x..)) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(scale = 2, rel_min_height = 0.01) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = log1p(c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000)),
    labels = scales::comma(c(0, 10, 100, 1000, 10000))
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#F8F5F0",
    high = "#325D88"
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0.0001, 0.5)) +
  labs(
    #title = "Distribution of Epidemiologically Linked Measles Cases by Region",
    #subtitle = "Country-year observations from 2012–2024",
    x = "Epidemiologically linked measles cases (log scale)",
    y = "",
    fill = "Cases"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

gdp_region <- cases_gdp_year |>
  ggplot(aes(x = gdp_per_capita,
             y = region,
             fill = ..x..)) +
  geom_density_ridges_gradient(scale = 2, rel_min_height = 0.01) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "#A6DAF5",
    high = "#3E3F3A"
  ) +
  scale_y_discrete(expand = c(0.0001, 0.5)) +
  labs(
    #title = "Distribution of GDP per Capita by Region",
    #subtitle = "Country-year observations from 2012–2024",
    x = "GDP per capita (log scale)",
    y = "",
    fill = "GDP per capita"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

epi_region_plot + gdp_region +
  plot_layout(ncol = 2) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Distributions of Measles Cases and GDP per Capita by Region",
    subtitle = "These plots show country-year distributions from 2012–2024",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 26),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 18)
    )
  )
